title: Visual feedback OSD overlay for volume/mode display
description:
# Description

Implement a visual on-screen display (OSD) overlay that appears when adjusting volume, showing the current level, mode, and state changes.

## Background & Rationale

### The Problem
Currently, users adjust volume "blind" — they rotate the dial but have no visual confirmation of:
- What the current volume level is
- Whether they're in Volume or Microphone mode
- Whether audio is muted
- How close they are to 0% or 100%

Every other volume control mechanism (keyboard media keys, TV remotes, phone volume buttons) provides immediate visual feedback. Without it, the Surface Dial feels like a toy rather than a professional tool.

### Why This Matters
1. **Reduces cognitive load** — No need to check system volume indicator separately
2. **Confirms mode** — Immediately see if you're adjusting volume vs mic
3. **Prevents errors** — See when you hit 0% or 100% boundaries
4. **Professional feel** — Polished UX that matches system-level controls
5. **Cross-platform consistency** — Same experience on all OSes

### Design Principles
- **Non-intrusive**: Small, translucent, auto-hides after inactivity
- **Fast**: Must appear within 16ms of first rotation event
- **Informative**: Show level, mode, mute state, and visual bar
- **Configurable**: Position, size, timeout, enable/disable

## Visual Design

```
┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│  🔊 Volume                      │
│  ████████████░░░░░░  65%        │
│                                 │
│  (or when in mic mode)          │
│  🎤 Microphone                  │
│  ██████░░░░░░░░░░░░  35%        │
│                                 │
│  (or when muted)                │
│  🔇 Muted                       │
│  ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  --         │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

- Translucent dark background (80% opacity)
- White text with slight shadow for readability
- Rounded corners (8px radius)
- Position: center-bottom by default (configurable)
- Size: approximately 200x60 pixels
- Auto-hide: fade out after 1.5 seconds of inactivity

## Platform Implementations

### macOS
```rust
// Use NSWindow with NSVisualEffectView for native blur
// Window level: NSScreenSaverWindowLevel (always on top)
// Style: NSWindowStyleMaskBorderless | NSWindowStyleMaskNonactivatingPanel

use cocoa::appkit::{NSWindow, NSWindowStyleMask};
use cocoa::base::nil;
use objc::runtime::Object;

fn create_osd_window() -> *mut Object {
    unsafe {
        let window = NSWindow::alloc(nil);
        window.initWithContentRect_styleMask_backing_defer_(
            NSRect::new(NSPoint::new(0., 0.), NSSize::new(200., 60.)),
            NSWindowStyleMask::NSBorderlessWindowMask,
            NSBackingStoreBuffered,
            NO,
        );
        window.setLevel_(NSScreenSaverWindowLevel);
        window.setOpaque_(NO);
        window.setBackgroundColor_(NSColor::clearColor(nil));
        window
    }
}
```

### Linux
```rust
// Option 1: GTK overlay window (Wayland + X11 compatible)
// Option 2: X11 override-redirect window (X11 only, simpler)

use gtk::prelude::*;

fn create_osd_window() -> gtk::Window {
    let window = gtk::Window::new(gtk::WindowType::Popup);
    window.set_decorated(false);
    window.set_keep_above(true);
    window.set_skip_taskbar_hint(true);
    window.set_skip_pager_hint(true);
    window.set_app_paintable(true);

    // Enable transparency
    if let Some(screen) = window.screen() {
        if let Some(visual) = screen.rgba_visual() {
            window.set_visual(Some(&visual));
        }
    }

    window
}
```

### Windows
```rust
// Use Win32 layered window with UpdateLayeredWindow
// Or WPF if we want easier styling

use windows::Win32::UI::WindowsAndMessaging::*;

fn create_osd_window() -> HWND {
    unsafe {
        let hwnd = CreateWindowExW(
            WS_EX_LAYERED | WS_EX_TRANSPARENT | WS_EX_TOPMOST | WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW,
            w!("STATIC"),
            w!("Surface Dial OSD"),
            WS_POPUP,
            0, 0, 200, 60,
            None, None, None, None,
        );
        SetLayeredWindowAttributes(hwnd, 0, 200, LWA_ALPHA);
        hwnd
    }
}
```

## Configuration Options

```toml
[osd]
enabled = true              # Enable/disable OSD
position = "center-bottom"  # center-bottom, center, top-right, etc.
size = "medium"             # small, medium, large
timeout_ms = 1500           # Auto-hide delay
opacity = 0.85              # Background opacity (0.0-1.0)
show_icon = true            # Show volume/mic emoji
show_percentage = true      # Show numeric percentage
```

## Integration Points

1. **Event trigger**: OSD shows on ANY rotation or mode change
2. **Update frequency**: Debounce to max 60fps (16ms)
3. **Hide trigger**: Start fade-out timer on last event
4. **Mode indication**: Different icon/color for volume vs mic
5. **Boundary indication**: Flash or highlight at 0% and 100%

## Dependencies

- Platform abstraction module (DIAL-9a4) - for cross-platform window creation
- Config storage (DIAL-mms) - for OSD settings
- Daemon refactor (DIAL-l6g) - integration point for events

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- Config parsing for OSD options
- Position calculation for different screen sizes
- Opacity/transparency math

### Integration Tests
- Window creation on each platform
- Event-to-display latency measurement
- Auto-hide timer accuracy

### E2E Tests
- Manual verification on each platform
- Screenshot comparison for visual regression
- Performance: CPU usage during continuous rotation

## Files to Create

- `src/osd/mod.rs` - OSD module and trait
- `src/osd/macos.rs` - macOS implementation
- `src/osd/linux.rs` - Linux implementation (GTK)
- `src/osd/windows.rs` - Windows implementation
- `src/osd/render.rs` - Shared rendering logic

## Success Criteria

- [ ] OSD appears within 50ms of first rotation
- [ ] Displays correct volume percentage
- [ ] Shows correct mode (Volume/Mic)
- [ ] Auto-hides after configured timeout
- [ ] Works on all three platforms
- [ ] CPU usage < 1% during idle
- [ ] Configurable via config file
- [ ] Can be disabled completely

title: macOS installer with launchd auto-start
description:
# Description

macOS-specific installer component that sets up launchd for auto-start on boot/login.

## Installation Steps

1. Download binary to `~/.local/bin/surface-dial`
2. Create launchd plist at `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.surface-dial.plist`
3. Load the launch agent
4. Verify daemon is running

## launchd Plist

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <key>Label</key>
    <string>com.surface-dial</string>
    
    <key>ProgramArguments</key>
    <array>
        <string>/Users/USERNAME/.local/bin/surface-dial</string>
        <string>daemon</string>
    </array>
    
    <key>RunAtLoad</key>
    <true/>
    
    <key>KeepAlive</key>
    <dict>
        <key>SuccessfulExit</key>
        <false/>
    </dict>
    
    <key>StandardOutPath</key>
    <string>/Users/USERNAME/.local/share/surface-dial/stdout.log</string>
    
    <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
    <string>/Users/USERNAME/.local/share/surface-dial/stderr.log</string>
    
    <key>ProcessType</key>
    <string>Background</string>
</dict>
</plist>
```

## Installer Script Section

```bash
install_macos() {
    local install_dir="${HOME}/.local/bin"
    local plist_dir="${HOME}/Library/LaunchAgents"
    local plist_file="${plist_dir}/com.surface-dial.plist"
    local log_dir="${HOME}/.local/share/surface-dial"
    
    # Create directories
    mkdir -p "$install_dir" "$plist_dir" "$log_dir"
    
    # Download binary
    download_binary "macos" "$install_dir/surface-dial"
    chmod +x "$install_dir/surface-dial"
    
    # Generate plist (substituting $HOME)
    generate_plist > "$plist_file"
    
    # Unload existing (if any)
    launchctl unload "$plist_file" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Load new
    launchctl load "$plist_file"
    
    log_info "✓ Installed and started"
    log_info "  Logs: $log_dir/"
}
```

## Uninstall Support

```bash
uninstall_macos() {
    launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.surface-dial.plist 2>/dev/null
    rm -f ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.surface-dial.plist
    rm -f ~/.local/bin/surface-dial
    # Keep config and logs (user data)
    log_info "✓ Uninstalled (config preserved)"
}
```

## Permissions

macOS requires Input Monitoring permission for HID access:
- System Preferences → Security & Privacy → Input Monitoring
- Add Terminal.app or the binary itself

Show user instructions if permission denied.

## Testing

```bash
# Install
./install.sh

# Verify
launchctl list | grep surface-dial
ps aux | grep surface-dial

# Logs
tail -f ~/.local/share/surface-dial/stderr.log
```
close_reason: Implemented simplified macOS installer: binary to ~/.local/bin, plist generated dynamically with proper log paths (~/.local/share/surface-dial/), no sudo required, ad-hoc signing for Apple Silicon, uninstall support via --uninstall flag. Updated uninstall.sh to handle both old and new installation paths.

description:
# Description

Add the `clap` crate to provide a proper CLI framework with subcommands for configuration.

## Implementation Details

```toml
# Add to Cargo.toml
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive", "env", "color"] }
toml = "0.8"  # For config file parsing
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
dirs = "5"   # For platform-specific config paths
```

## CLI Structure

```
surface-dial                     # Run daemon (default)
surface-dial --help              # Show quick-start guide
surface-dial daemon              # Run daemon explicitly
surface-dial config              # Show all settings
surface-dial config get <key>    # Get specific setting
surface-dial config set <key> <value>  # Set setting
surface-dial config reset        # Reset to defaults
surface-dial status              # Check if daemon running, dial connected
surface-dial version             # Show version info
```

## Subcommand Patterns

Each subcommand should be a separate module in `src/cli/`:
- `src/cli/mod.rs` - CLI definitions
- `src/cli/daemon.rs` - Daemon runner
- `src/cli/config.rs` - Config management  
- `src/cli/status.rs` - Status checks

## Agent-Friendly Design

- All commands should support `--json` for machine-readable output
- Exit codes: 0=success, 1=error, 2=invalid args
- Stderr for errors, stdout for data

## Files to Modify

- `Cargo.toml` - Add dependencies
- `src/main.rs` - Replace arg parsing with clap
- Create `src/cli/` module structure

## Testing

```bash
cargo run -- --help
cargo run -- config
cargo run -- config set volume.step.max 10 --json
```
close_reason: Implemented clap CLI framework with subcommands: daemon, config (show/get/set/reset/path), status, version. Added --json, --verbose, --quiet global flags. 83 tests passing.

description:
# Description

End-to-end test scripts that verify the complete system works on each platform, including all new features.

## Test Script Structure

```
tests/
├── e2e/
│   ├── run_all.sh           # Cross-platform test runner
│   ├── common.sh            # Shared functions and logging
│   ├── test_install.sh      # Installation tests
│   ├── test_daemon.sh       # Daemon lifecycle tests
│   ├── test_config.sh       # Configuration tests
│   ├── test_cli.sh          # CLI command tests
│   ├── test_osd.sh          # OSD overlay tests
│   ├── test_battery.sh      # Battery monitoring tests
│   ├── test_volume_curves.sh    # Volume curve tests
│   ├── test_sensitivity.sh  # Sensitivity/dead zone tests
│   ├── test_media_control.sh    # Triple-click media tests
│   ├── test_device_switching.sh # Device switching tests
│   ├── test_tray.sh         # System tray tests
│   ├── test_webhooks.sh     # Event webhook tests
│   ├── test_audio_feedback.sh   # Audio tick tests
│   └── fixtures/
│       ├── config_valid.toml
│       ├── config_invalid.toml
│       ├── config_all_features.toml
│       └── mock_webhook_server.py
```

## Logging Framework

```bash
# common.sh - Comprehensive logging framework

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# === Colors ===
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
GRAY='\033[0;90m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# === Log File Setup ===
LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-/tmp/surface-dial-tests}"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
LOG_FILE="$LOG_DIR/e2e_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
SUMMARY_FILE="$LOG_DIR/summary_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).txt"

# === Counters ===
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0
TESTS_SKIPPED=0
CURRENT_TEST=""
CURRENT_SUITE=""

# === Logging Functions ===
log() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local msg="$*"
    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N')
    
    # Write to log file (plain)
    echo "[$timestamp] [$level] $msg" >> "$LOG_FILE"
    
    # Write to console (colored)
    case "$level" in
        DEBUG) echo -e "${GRAY}[$timestamp] [DEBUG] $msg${NC}" ;;
        INFO)  echo -e "${BLUE}[$timestamp] [INFO]  $msg${NC}" ;;
        STEP)  echo -e "${CYAN}[$timestamp] [STEP]  $msg${NC}" ;;
        PASS)  echo -e "${GREEN}[$timestamp] [PASS]  $msg${NC}" ;;
        FAIL)  echo -e "${RED}[$timestamp] [FAIL]  $msg${NC}" ;;
        WARN)  echo -e "${YELLOW}[$timestamp] [WARN]  $msg${NC}" ;;
        *)     echo "[$timestamp] [$level] $msg" ;;
    esac
}

debug() { log DEBUG "$@"; }
info()  { log INFO "$@"; }
step()  { log STEP "$@"; }
pass()  { log PASS "$@"; }
fail()  { log FAIL "$@"; }
warn()  { log WARN "$@"; }

# === Test Management ===
suite_start() {
    CURRENT_SUITE="$1"
    info "=========================================="
    info "Starting test suite: $CURRENT_SUITE"
    info "=========================================="
}

test_start() {
    CURRENT_TEST="$1"
    step "Test: $CURRENT_TEST"
}

test_pass() {
    pass "✓ $CURRENT_TEST"
    ((TESTS_PASSED++))
}

test_fail() {
    local reason="${1:-Test failed}"
    fail "✗ $CURRENT_TEST: $reason"
    ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    echo "$CURRENT_SUITE::$CURRENT_TEST: FAILED - $reason" >> "$SUMMARY_FILE"
}

test_skip() {
    local reason="${1:-Skipped}"
    warn "⊘ $CURRENT_TEST: $reason"
    ((TESTS_SKIPPED++))
}

# === Assertions ===
assert_eq() {
    local expected="$1"
    local actual="$2"
    local msg="${3:-Values should be equal}"
    
    if [[ "$expected" == "$actual" ]]; then
        debug "assert_eq passed: '$expected' == '$actual'"
        return 0
    else
        fail "$msg: expected '$expected', got '$actual'"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_contains() {
    local haystack="$1"
    local needle="$2"
    local msg="${3:-Should contain substring}"
    
    if [[ "$haystack" == *"$needle"* ]]; then
        debug "assert_contains passed: found '$needle'"
        return 0
    else
        fail "$msg: '$needle' not found in output"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_file_exists() {
    local path="$1"
    local msg="${2:-File should exist}"
    
    if [[ -f "$path" ]]; then
        debug "assert_file_exists passed: $path"
        return 0
    else
        fail "$msg: file not found: $path"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_exit_code() {
    local expected="$1"
    local actual="$2"
    local msg="${3:-Exit code mismatch}"
    
    if [[ "$expected" == "$actual" ]]; then
        debug "assert_exit_code passed: $actual"
        return 0
    else
        fail "$msg: expected exit code $expected, got $actual"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_json_valid() {
    local json="$1"
    local msg="${2:-Should be valid JSON}"
    
    if echo "$json" | jq . >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        debug "assert_json_valid passed"
        return 0
    else
        fail "$msg: invalid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_within_time() {
    local max_ms="$1"
    local start_time="$2"
    local end_time="$3"
    local msg="${4:-Should complete within time limit}"
    
    local elapsed=$((end_time - start_time))
    if [[ $elapsed -le $max_ms ]]; then
        debug "assert_within_time passed: ${elapsed}ms <= ${max_ms}ms"
        return 0
    else
        fail "$msg: took ${elapsed}ms, limit was ${max_ms}ms"
        return 1
    fi
}

# === Cleanup ===
cleanup() {
    info "Cleaning up test environment..."
    # Kill any test daemons
    pkill -f "surface-dial.*test" 2>/dev/null || true
    # Remove temp configs
    rm -rf "${TEST_CONFIG_DIR:-/tmp/sd-test-config}" 2>/dev/null || true
}

trap cleanup EXIT

# === Summary ===
print_summary() {
    echo ""
    info "=========================================="
    info "Test Summary"
    info "=========================================="
    info "Passed:  $TESTS_PASSED"
    info "Failed:  $TESTS_FAILED"
    info "Skipped: $TESTS_SKIPPED"
    info "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
    
    if [[ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
        fail "Some tests failed!"
        exit 1
    else
        pass "All tests passed!"
        exit 0
    fi
}

# === Binary Path ===
BINARY="${BINARY:-./target/release/surface-dial}"
export TEST_CONFIG_DIR="/tmp/sd-test-config"
export XDG_CONFIG_HOME="$TEST_CONFIG_DIR"
```

## Test Suites

### 1. Installation Tests (test_install.sh)

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
source "$(dirname "$0")/common.sh"

suite_start "Installation Tests"

test_start "Fresh install to temp directory"
{
    temp_dir=$(mktemp -d)
    export PREFIX="$temp_dir"
    ./install.sh
    
    assert_file_exists "$temp_dir/bin/surface-dial" "Binary installed"
    
    # Verify binary runs
    "$temp_dir/bin/surface-dial" --version
    assert_exit_code 0 $?
    
    rm -rf "$temp_dir"
    test_pass
} || test_fail

test_start "Platform auto-detection"
{
    output=$(./install.sh --detect-only)
    case "$(uname)" in
        Darwin) assert_contains "$output" "macos" ;;
        Linux)  assert_contains "$output" "linux" ;;
        MINGW*|MSYS*) assert_contains "$output" "windows" ;;
    esac
    test_pass
} || test_fail

print_summary
```

### 2. Daemon Lifecycle Tests (test_daemon.sh)

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
source "$(dirname "$0")/common.sh"

suite_start "Daemon Lifecycle Tests"

test_start "Daemon starts and creates PID file"
{
    $BINARY daemon start &
    sleep 2
    
    pid_file="$TEST_CONFIG_DIR/surface-dial/daemon.pid"
    assert_file_exists "$pid_file"
    
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")
    kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null
    assert_exit_code 0 $?
    
    $BINARY daemon stop
    test_pass
} || test_fail

test_start "Daemon graceful shutdown on SIGTERM"
{
    $BINARY daemon start &
    sleep 1
    pid=$($BINARY status --json | jq -r '.daemon.pid')
    
    kill -TERM "$pid"
    sleep 1
    
    # Should have exited cleanly
    ! kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null
    assert_exit_code 0 $?
    test_pass
} || test_fail

test_start "Status command shows accurate state"
{
    # Daemon not running
    output=$($BINARY status --json)
    assert_json_valid "$output"
    assert_contains "$output" '"running":false'
    
    # Start daemon
    $BINARY daemon start &
    sleep 2
    
    output=$($BINARY status --json)
    assert_contains "$output" '"running":true'
    
    $BINARY daemon stop
    test_pass
} || test_fail

print_summary
```

### 3. Configuration Tests (test_config.sh)

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
source "$(dirname "$0")/common.sh"

suite_start "Configuration Tests"

test_start "Default config creation on first run"
{
    rm -rf "$TEST_CONFIG_DIR"
    
    # Any command that reads config should create defaults
    $BINARY config get volume.step_min
    
    config_file="$TEST_CONFIG_DIR/surface-dial/config.toml"
    assert_file_exists "$config_file"
    test_pass
} || test_fail

    $BINARY config set volume.step_max 12
    output=$($BINARY config get volume.step_max)
    assert_eq "12" "$output"
    
    # Verify in file
    config_file="$TEST_CONFIG_DIR/surface-dial/config.toml"
    assert_contains "$(cat "$config_file")" "step_max = 12"
    test_pass
} || test_fail

test_start "Invalid values rejected with helpful error"
{
    output=$($BINARY config set volume.step_min 100 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$output" "must be"
    
    # Value should be unchanged
    current=$($BINARY config get volume.step_min)
    assert_eq "2" "$current"
    test_pass
} || test_fail

test_start "Config reset restores defaults"
{
    $BINARY config set volume.step_max 15
    $BINARY config reset
    
    value=$($BINARY config get volume.step_max)
    assert_eq "8" "$value"
    test_pass
} || test_fail

test_start "All feature configs have defaults"
{
    rm -rf "$TEST_CONFIG_DIR"
    
    # Check each feature config exists
    for key in "osd.enabled" "battery.enabled" "tray.enabled" "sensitivity.dead_zone"; do
        value=$($BINARY config get "$key")
        [[ -n "$value" ]] || { fail "Missing config: $key"; return 1; }
    done
    test_pass
} || test_fail

print_summary
```

### 4. OSD Tests (test_osd.sh)

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
source "$(dirname "$0")/common.sh"

suite_start "OSD Overlay Tests"

test_start "OSD can be enabled/disabled via config"
{
    $BINARY config set osd.enabled true
    value=$($BINARY config get osd.enabled)
    assert_eq "true" "$value"
    
    $BINARY config set osd.enabled false
    value=$($BINARY config get osd.enabled)
    assert_eq "false" "$value"
    test_pass
} || test_fail

test_start "OSD timeout is configurable"
{
    $BINARY config set osd.timeout_ms 2000
    value=$($BINARY config get osd.timeout_ms)
    assert_eq "2000" "$value"
    test_pass
} || test_fail

test_start "OSD position options are valid"
{
    for pos in "center-bottom" "center" "top-right" "bottom-left"; do
        $BINARY config set osd.position "$pos"
        value=$($BINARY config get osd.position)
        assert_eq "$pos" "$value"
    done
    test_pass
} || test_fail

# Note: Actual OSD display requires manual verification or screenshot comparison
test_start "OSD window creation (platform-specific)"
{
    case "$(uname)" in
        Darwin)
            # macOS: Check if we can create NSWindow
            test_pass
            ;;
        Linux)
            # Linux: Check GTK availability
            if command -v gtk-launch >/dev/null; then
                test_pass
            else
                test_skip "GTK not available"
            fi
            ;;
        *)
            test_skip "Platform not yet tested"
            ;;

} || test_fail

print_summary
```

### 5. Volume Curve Tests (test_volume_curves.sh)

```bash

title: Event Webhooks and Script Triggers
Create unified install.sh with platform auto-detection
description:
# Description

Create a single install.sh script that auto-detects the platform and runs the appropriate installer.

## Design (giil-style)

The script should:
1. Detect OS (macOS/Linux/Windows via WSL)
2. Detect architecture (x86_64/aarch64)
3. Download appropriate binary
4. Install to ~/.local/bin
5. Set up auto-start service
6. Show beautiful completion message

## Script Structure

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Surface Dial Volume Controller Installer
# 
# One-line install:
#   curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/USER/surface-dial/main/install.sh | bash
#
# Options:
#   DEST=/path        Install directory (default: ~/.local/bin)
#   NO_AUTOSTART=1    Skip auto-start setup
#   VERSION=x.y.z     Install specific version

set -euo pipefail

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
NC='\033[0m'

REPO="USER/surface-dial"
BINARY_NAME="surface-dial"

log_info() { echo -e "${GREEN}[installer]${NC} $1"; }
log_warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[installer]${NC} $1"; }
log_error() { echo -e "${RED}[installer]${NC} $1"; }
log_step() { echo -e "${BLUE}[installer]${NC} $1"; }

detect_platform() {
    local os arch
    case "$(uname -s)" in
        Darwin) os="macos" ;;
        Linux)  os="linux" ;;
        MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*) os="windows" ;;
        *) log_error "Unsupported OS: $(uname -s)"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
    case "$(uname -m)" in
        x86_64|amd64) arch="x86_64" ;;
        aarch64|arm64) arch="aarch64" ;;
        *) log_error "Unsupported arch: $(uname -m)"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
    echo "${os}_${arch}"
}

download_binary() {
    local platform="$1"
    local dest="$2"
    local version="${VERSION:-latest}"
    local url
    
    if [[ "$version" == "latest" ]]; then
        url="https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/latest/download/${BINARY_NAME}-${platform}"
    else
        url="https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/download/v${version}/${BINARY_NAME}-${platform}"
    fi
    
    if [[ "$platform" == windows_* ]]; then
        url="${url}.exe"
    fi
    
    log_step "Downloading from ${url}..."
    
    if command -v curl &>/dev/null; then
        curl -fsSL "$url" -o "$dest"
    elif command -v wget &>/dev/null; then
        wget -qO "$dest" "$url"
    else
        log_error "curl or wget required"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    chmod +x "$dest"
}

setup_autostart_macos() {
    local binary_path="$1"
    local plist_dir="${HOME}/Library/LaunchAgents"
    local plist="${plist_dir}/com.surface-dial.plist"
    
    mkdir -p "$plist_dir"
    
    cat > "$plist" << PLIST
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <key>Label</key><string>com.surface-dial</string>
    <key>ProgramArguments</key><array><string>${binary_path}</string><string>daemon</string></array>
    <key>RunAtLoad</key><true/>
    <key>KeepAlive</key><dict><key>SuccessfulExit</key><false/></dict>
</dict>
</plist>
PLIST
    
    launchctl unload "$plist" 2>/dev/null || true
    launchctl load "$plist"
    log_info "Auto-start configured (launchd)"
}

setup_autostart_linux() {
    local binary_path="$1"
    local service_dir="${HOME}/.config/systemd/user"
    local service="${service_dir}/surface-dial.service"
    
    mkdir -p "$service_dir"
    
    cat > "$service" << SERVICE
[Unit]
Description=Surface Dial Volume Controller
After=bluetooth.target

[Service]
ExecStart=${binary_path} daemon
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
SERVICE
    
    systemctl --user daemon-reload
    systemctl --user enable --now surface-dial.service
    log_info "Auto-start configured (systemd)"
}

main() {
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗${NC}"
    echo -e "${BOLD}║${NC}  🎛️  ${BOLD}Surface Dial Volume Controller${NC}                          ${BOLD}║${NC}"
    echo -e "${BOLD}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝${NC}"
    echo ""
    
    local platform install_dir binary_path
    platform=$(detect_platform)
    install_dir="${DEST:-${HOME}/.local/bin}"
    binary_path="${install_dir}/${BINARY_NAME}"
    
    log_step "Platform: ${platform}"
    log_step "Install to: ${install_dir}"
    
    mkdir -p "$install_dir"
    download_binary "$platform" "$binary_path"
    
    if [[ -z "${NO_AUTOSTART:-}" ]]; then
        case "$platform" in
            macos_*) setup_autostart_macos "$binary_path" ;;
            linux_*) setup_autostart_linux "$binary_path" ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    echo ""
    log_info "✓ Installation complete!"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}Controls:${NC}"
    echo -e "  ${GREEN}↻${NC} Rotate          Adjust volume"
    echo -e "  ${GREEN}⏺${NC} Click           Toggle mute"
    echo -e "  ${GREEN}⏺⏺${NC} Double-click    Mic mode (10s)"
    echo -e "  ${GREEN}⏸${NC} Hold 1s         Send F15"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}Commands:${NC}"
    echo -e "  ${binary_path} status"
    echo -e "  ${binary_path} config"
    echo ""
}

main "$@"
```

## Files to Create

- `install.sh` - Main installer script
- `install.ps1` - Windows PowerShell installer (separate task)

# Test options
DEST=/tmp/test NO_AUTOSTART=1 ./install.sh
```

title: GitHub Actions cross-platform release workflow
description:
# Description

Create GitHub Actions workflow that builds releases for all platforms on tag push.

## Workflow File

```yaml
# .github/workflows/release.yml
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build \${{ matrix.name }}
    runs-on: \${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          # macOS Apple Silicon
          - os: macos-latest
            target: aarch64-apple-darwin
            name: macos-aarch64
            ext: ""
          # macOS Intel
          - os: macos-latest
            target: x86_64-apple-darwin
            name: macos-x86_64
            ext: ""
          # Linux x64
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            name: linux-x86_64
            ext: ""
          # Linux ARM64
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            name: linux-aarch64
            ext: ""
          # Windows
          - os: windows-latest
            target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
            name: windows-x86_64
            ext: ".exe"

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
        with:
          targets: \${{ matrix.target }}
      
      - name: Install cross-compilation tools (Linux ARM64)
        if: matrix.target == 'aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu
      
      - name: Install hidapi dependencies (Linux)
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        run: sudo apt-get install -y libudev-dev libhidapi-dev
      
      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --release --target \${{ matrix.target }}
      
      - name: Package
        shell: bash
        run: |
          mkdir -p dist
          cp target/\${{ matrix.target }}/release/surface-dial\${{ matrix.ext }} \
             dist/surface-dial-\${{ matrix.name }}\${{ matrix.ext }}
      
      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: surface-dial-\${{ matrix.name }}
          path: dist/surface-dial-\${{ matrix.name }}*

  release:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Download all artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: dist
          merge-multiple: true
      
      - name: Generate checksums
        run: |
          cd dist
          sha256sum * > checksums.txt
      
      - name: Create Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
        with:
          files: |
            dist/*
          body: |
            ## Installation
            
            ### macOS / Linux
            \`\`\`bash
            curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/\${{ github.repository }}/main/install.sh | bash
            \`\`\`
            
            ### Windows (PowerShell)
            \`\`\`powershell
            irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/\${{ github.repository }}/main/install.ps1 | iex
            \`\`\`
            
            ## Binaries
            
            | Platform | Binary |
            |----------|--------|
            | macOS (Apple Silicon) | surface-dial-macos-aarch64 |
            | macOS (Intel) | surface-dial-macos-x86_64 |
            | Linux (x64) | surface-dial-linux-x86_64 |
            | Linux (ARM64) | surface-dial-linux-aarch64 |
            | Windows | surface-dial-windows-x86_64.exe |
          draft: false
          prerelease: false
```

## Release Process

1. Update version in Cargo.toml
2. Commit: `git commit -am "Release v0.2.0"`
3. Tag: `git tag v0.2.0`
4. Push: `git push && git push --tags`
5. GitHub Actions builds and publishes release

## Cross-Compilation Notes

### Linux ARM64
- Uses `cross` or `gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu` 
- May need QEMU for testing

### macOS Universal Binary (Optional)
```yaml
- name: Create Universal Binary
  if: runner.os == 'macOS'
  run: |
    lipo -create \
      target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/surface-dial \
      target/x86_64-apple-darwin/release/surface-dial \
      -output dist/surface-dial-macos-universal
```

## Files to Create

- `.github/workflows/release.yml` - Release workflow
- Consider: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` - PR checks

## Testing

- Push a tag to trigger workflow
- Verify all 5 binaries are built
- Download and test on each platform

title: CI workflow for tests (unit, integration, e2e)
description:
# Description

GitHub Actions workflow that runs all test suites on every PR and push, with cross-platform support and detailed logging.

## Workflow File

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1

jobs:
  check:
    name: Format & Lint
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
        with:
          components: rustfmt, clippy
      
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      
      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --all -- --check
      
      - name: Run clippy
        run: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

  unit-tests:
    name: Unit Tests (${{ matrix.os }})
    needs: check
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
      
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          key: ${{ matrix.os }}
      
      - name: Install Linux dependencies
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y libudev-dev libgtk-3-dev libappindicator3-dev libasound2-dev
      
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: cargo test --lib --verbose
        env:
          RUST_LOG: debug
      
      - name: Upload test logs on failure
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: unit-test-logs-${{ matrix.os }}
          path: |
            target/debug/.fingerprint/*/output
            **/*.log

  integration-tests:
    name: Integration Tests (${{ matrix.os }})
    needs: unit-tests
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
      
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          key: ${{ matrix.os }}-integration
      
      - name: Install Linux dependencies
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y libudev-dev libgtk-3-dev libappindicator3-dev libasound2-dev
      
      - name: Run integration tests
        run: cargo test --test integration --verbose
        env:
          RUST_LOG: debug
          SURFACE_DIAL_TEST_MODE: mock
      
      - name: Upload test logs on failure
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: integration-test-logs-${{ matrix.os }}
          path: |
            **/*.log
            target/debug/deps/*.log

  e2e-tests:
    name: E2E Tests (${{ matrix.os }})
    needs: integration-tests
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
      
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          key: ${{ matrix.os }}-e2e
      
      - name: Install Linux dependencies
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y libudev-dev libgtk-3-dev libappindicator3-dev libasound2-dev
      
      - name: Build release binary
        run: cargo build --release
      
      - name: Run E2E tests (Unix)
        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
        run: |
          chmod +x tests/e2e/*.sh
          ./tests/e2e/run_all.sh
        env:
          SURFACE_DIAL_BIN: ./target/release/surface-dial
          SURFACE_DIAL_TEST_MODE: mock
          LOG_LEVEL: debug
      
      - name: Run E2E tests (Windows)
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $env:SURFACE_DIAL_BIN = ".\target\release\surface-dial.exe"
          $env:SURFACE_DIAL_TEST_MODE = "mock"
          $env:LOG_LEVEL = "debug"
          .\tests\e2e\run_all.ps1
      
      - name: Upload E2E test logs
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: e2e-test-logs-${{ matrix.os }}
          path: |
            tests/e2e/logs/
            /tmp/surface-dial-test-*.log

  coverage:
    name: Code Coverage
    needs: unit-tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
        with:
          components: llvm-tools-preview
      
      - name: Install cargo-llvm-cov
        uses: taiki-e/install-action@cargo-llvm-cov
      
      - name: Install Linux dependencies
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y libudev-dev libgtk-3-dev libappindicator3-dev libasound2-dev
      
      - name: Generate coverage report
        run: cargo llvm-cov --all-features --lcov --output-path lcov.info
      
      - name: Upload to Codecov
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          files: lcov.info
          fail_ci_if_error: true
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  all-tests:
    name: All Tests Pass
    needs: [check, unit-tests, integration-tests, e2e-tests]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: All tests passed
        run: echo "All test suites passed successfully!"

Linux installer with systemd user service
description:
# Description

Linux-specific installer that sets up a systemd user service for auto-start.

## Installation Steps

1. Download binary to `~/.local/bin/surface-dial`
2. Create udev rule for Surface Dial HID access (optional, if needed)
3. Create systemd user service
4. Enable and start the service

## Systemd User Service

```ini
# ~/.config/systemd/user/surface-dial.service
[Unit]
Description=Surface Dial Volume Controller
Documentation=https://github.com/USER/surface-dial
After=default.target bluetooth.target

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=%h/.local/bin/surface-dial daemon
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5
Environment=RUST_LOG=info

# Hardening
NoNewPrivileges=yes
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=read-only
ReadWritePaths=%h/.config/surface-dial %h/.local/share/surface-dial

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

## udev Rule (if needed for permissions)

```
# /etc/udev/rules.d/99-surface-dial.rules
# Allow non-root access to Surface Dial HID device
SUBSYSTEM=="hidraw", ATTRS{idVendor}=="045e", ATTRS{idProduct}=="091b", MODE="0666"
```

## Installer Script Section

```bash
install_linux() {
    local install_dir="${HOME}/.local/bin"
    local service_dir="${HOME}/.config/systemd/user"
    local service_file="${service_dir}/surface-dial.service"
    
    # Create directories
    mkdir -p "$install_dir" "$service_dir"
    
    # Download binary
    download_binary "linux" "$install_dir/surface-dial"
    chmod +x "$install_dir/surface-dial"
    
    # Generate service file
    generate_systemd_service > "$service_file"
    
    # Reload systemd
    systemctl --user daemon-reload
    
    # Enable and start
    systemctl --user enable surface-dial.service
    systemctl --user start surface-dial.service
    
    # Check udev permissions
    check_hid_permissions
    
    log_info "✓ Installed and started"
    log_info "  Status: systemctl --user status surface-dial"
}

check_hid_permissions() {
    # Check if we can access Surface Dial HID device
    if ! ls /dev/hidraw* 2>/dev/null | xargs -I{} test -r {} 2>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "HID permissions may need configuration"
        log_warn "Run with sudo to install udev rule, or see docs"
    fi
}
```

## Uninstall Support

```bash
uninstall_linux() {
    systemctl --user stop surface-dial.service 2>/dev/null
    systemctl --user disable surface-dial.service 2>/dev/null
    rm -f ~/.config/systemd/user/surface-dial.service
    rm -f ~/.local/bin/surface-dial
    systemctl --user daemon-reload
    log_info "✓ Uninstalled (config preserved)"
}
```

## Testing

```bash
# Install
./install.sh

# Verify
systemctl --user status surface-dial
journalctl --user -u surface-dial -f

# Manual run
~/.local/bin/surface-dial daemon
```

## Notes

- User services require `loginctl enable-linger $USER` for running without login
- Some distros may need additional HID permission setup

description:
# Description

Windows-specific installer (PowerShell) that sets up Task Scheduler for auto-start on login.

## PowerShell Installer (install.ps1)

```powershell
#Requires -Version 5.1
<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Surface Dial Volume Controller Installer for Windows
.DESCRIPTION
    Downloads and installs surface-dial.exe with auto-start on login
.PARAMETER InstallDir
    Installation directory (default: $env:LOCALAPPDATA\surface-dial)
.PARAMETER NoAutoStart
    Skip Task Scheduler setup
#>

param(
    [string]$InstallDir = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\surface-dial",
    [switch]$NoAutoStart
)

$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"

# Colors
function Write-Info { Write-Host "[installer] $args" -ForegroundColor Green }
function Write-Warn { Write-Host "[installer] $args" -ForegroundColor Yellow }
function Write-Err { Write-Host "[installer] $args" -ForegroundColor Red }

# Banner
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "║  🎛️  Surface Dial Volume Controller - Windows Installer       ║" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

# Create directory
if (-not (Test-Path $InstallDir)) {
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $InstallDir -Force | Out-Null
}

# Download binary
$exePath = Join-Path $InstallDir "surface-dial.exe"
$downloadUrl = "https://github.com/USER/surface-dial/releases/latest/download/surface-dial-windows-x86_64.exe"
Write-Info "Downloading..."
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $downloadUrl -OutFile $exePath

# Task Scheduler
if (-not $NoAutoStart) {
    Write-Info "Setting up auto-start..."
    
    $taskName = "SurfaceDialController"
    $action = New-ScheduledTaskAction -Execute $exePath -Argument "daemon"
    $trigger = New-ScheduledTaskTrigger -AtLogon
    $settings = New-ScheduledTaskSettingsSet -AllowStartIfOnBatteries -DontStopIfGoingOnBatteries
    
    # Remove existing task if present
    Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    
    # Create new task
    Register-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -Action $action -Trigger $trigger -Settings $settings | Out-Null
    
    # Start immediately
    Start-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName
}

Write-Host ""
Write-Info "✓ Installation complete!"
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "  Installed to: $exePath" -ForegroundColor Blue
Write-Host "  Auto-start:   Task Scheduler ($taskName)" -ForegroundColor Blue
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "  Commands:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host "    $exePath status       Check daemon status"
Write-Host "    $exePath config       Show configuration"
Write-Host ""
```

## Uninstall Support

```powershell
function Uninstall-SurfaceDial {
    $taskName = "SurfaceDialController"
    $installDir = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\surface-dial"
    
    # Stop and remove task
    Stop-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    
    # Remove binary
    Remove-Item -Path "$installDir\surface-dial.exe" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    
    Write-Info "✓ Uninstalled (config preserved)"
}
```

## HID Access on Windows

Windows generally allows user-space HID access via:
- WinRT APIs (recommended)
- `hidapi` crate with Windows backend

No special permissions needed for standard HID devices.

## Testing

```powershell
# Install
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/USER/surface-dial/main/install.ps1 | iex

# Or with options
.\install.ps1 -InstallDir "C:\tools\surface-dial" -NoAutoStart

# Verify
Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName "SurfaceDialController"
Get-Process surface-dial
```

## Files to Create

- `install.ps1` - PowerShell installer
- Document in README for Windows users

title: README.md with installation and usage guide
description:
# Description

Comprehensive README.md with installation instructions, usage guide, feature documentation, and troubleshooting.

## README Structure

```markdown
# Surface Dial Volume Controller

Control system volume with your Microsoft Surface Dial on macOS, Linux, and Windows.

![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue.svg)
![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-lightgrey)

## Features

### Core Features
- **Volume Control** - Rotate to adjust system volume with configurable curves
- **Mute Toggle** - Single-click to toggle mute
- **Mic Control Mode** - Double-click for 10-second microphone control
- **Media Control** - Triple-click for Play/Pause
- **F15 Key** - Long-press (1 sec) to hold F15 key
- **Acceleration** - Faster rotation = bigger steps

### Advanced Features
- **Volume Curves** - Linear, logarithmic, exponential, or custom curves
- **Visual OSD** - On-screen display showing volume level and mode
- **Battery Monitoring** - Low battery notifications
- **Device Switching** - Long-press + rotate to switch audio outputs
- **Per-App Volume** - Control foreground application volume (Linux/Windows)
- **System Tray** - Status icon with quick access menu
- **Audio Feedback** - Optional tick sounds for haptic-like feedback
- **Sensitivity Settings** - Dead zone and multiplier for accessibility
- **Event Hooks** - Script triggers and webhooks for home automation

### Cross-Platform
- **Auto-Start** - Runs on boot with native service (launchd/systemd/Task Scheduler)
- **Same Experience** - Identical UX across macOS, Linux, and Windows

## Quick Install

### macOS / Linux
\`\`\`bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/USER/surface-dial/main/install.sh | bash
\`\`\`

### Windows (PowerShell as Admin)
\`\`\`powershell
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/USER/surface-dial/main/install.ps1 | iex
\`\`\`

## Usage

### Controls Reference

| Input | Action |
|-------|--------|
| Rotate | Adjust volume (or mic in mic mode) |
| Single-click | Toggle mute |
| Double-click | Switch to mic control for 10 seconds |
| Triple-click | Play/Pause media |
| Long-press (1s) | Send F15 key (hold until release) |
| Long-press + rotate | Cycle through audio output devices |

### CLI Commands

\`\`\`bash
surface-dial                           # Show quick-start guide
surface-dial daemon                    # Run in foreground
surface-dial status                    # Check daemon/device status
surface-dial status --json             # Machine-readable status

surface-dial config                    # Show all settings
surface-dial config get volume.curve   # Get specific setting
surface-dial config set volume.curve logarithmic  # Update setting
surface-dial config reset              # Reset to defaults

surface-dial logs                      # View recent logs
surface-dial logs --follow             # Watch logs in real-time
surface-dial logs --json               # JSON format for parsing
\`\`\`

### Configuration

Config file location:
- **macOS**: `~/Library/Application Support/surface-dial/config.toml`
- **Linux**: `~/.config/surface-dial/config.toml`
- **Windows**: `%APPDATA%\surface-dial\config.toml`

#### Complete Configuration Reference

\`\`\`toml
[volume]
step_min = 2                  # Slow rotation step (1-20)
step_max = 8                  # Fast rotation step (1-20)
curve = "logarithmic"         # linear, logarithmic, exponential, custom
curve_steepness = 9.0         # For log/exp curves (1.0-20.0)

[microphone]
step_min = 3
step_max = 10
mode_duration = 10            # Seconds in mic mode

[acceleration]
fast_ms = 80                  # Fast spin threshold
slow_ms = 400                 # Slow spin threshold

[interaction]
double_click_ms = 400
triple_click_ms = 600
long_press_ms = 1000

[sensitivity]
dead_zone = 0                 # 0-10, ignore small movements
multiplier = 1.0              # 0.1-5.0, sensitivity scale
invert = false                # Reverse rotation direction

[osd]
enabled = true
position = "center-bottom"    # center-bottom, center, top-right, etc.
timeout_ms = 1500
opacity = 0.85

[battery]
enabled = true
poll_interval_seconds = 300
warning_thresholds = [20, 10, 5]

[device_switching]
enabled = true
mode = "long_press_rotate"

[media_control]
enabled = true
triple_click_action = "play_pause"

[audio_feedback]
enabled = false               # Disabled by default
volume = 0.3
tick = true
boundary = true

[tray]
enabled = true
show_volume = true
show_battery = true

[events]
enabled = false
debounce_ms = 500

# [[events.scripts]]
# events = ["volume_change"]
# script = "~/.config/surface-dial/scripts/my-script.sh"

[daemon]
log_level = "info"            # error, warn, info, debug, trace
log_file_enabled = true
log_max_size_mb = 10
log_keep_files = 3
\`\`\`

## Troubleshooting

### Device not detected

1. Ensure Surface Dial is paired via Bluetooth
2. Wake the dial (press button - it sleeps when idle)
3. Check status: `surface-dial status`
4. Look for HID device: 
   - macOS: System Information → Bluetooth
   - Linux: `lsusb` or `bluetoothctl info`
   - Windows: Device Manager → Bluetooth

### Permission denied (macOS)

macOS requires Input Monitoring permission:
1. System Preferences → Security & Privacy → Privacy → Input Monitoring
2. Add Terminal.app (or the binary itself)
3. Restart the daemon

### Permission denied (Linux)

Add udev rule for non-root HID access:
\`\`\`bash
echo 'SUBSYSTEM=="hidraw", ATTRS{idVendor}=="045e", ATTRS{idProduct}=="091b", MODE="0666", GROUP="plugdev"' | sudo tee /etc/udev/rules.d/99-surface-dial.rules
sudo udevadm control --reload-rules
sudo usermod -aG plugdev $USER
# Log out and back in
\`\`\`

### Daemon not starting on boot

Check service status:
\`\`\`bash
# macOS
launchctl list | grep surface-dial

# Linux
systemctl --user status surface-dial

# Windows (PowerShell)
Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName "SurfaceDial"
\`\`\`

### View logs

\`\`\`bash
surface-dial logs --follow

# Or directly:
# macOS: tail -f ~/Library/Application\ Support/surface-dial/daemon.log
# Linux: journalctl --user -u surface-dial -f
# Windows: type %APPDATA%\surface-dial\daemon.log
\`\`\`

## Building from Source

### Prerequisites
- Rust 1.75+ (install from rustup.rs)
- Platform-specific dependencies:
  - **macOS**: Xcode Command Line Tools
  - **Linux**: `libudev-dev libgtk-3-dev libappindicator3-dev`
  - **Windows**: Visual Studio Build Tools

### Build

\`\`\`bash
git clone https://github.com/USER/surface-dial
cd surface-dial
cargo build --release
./target/release/surface-dial --help
\`\`\`

### Run Tests

\`\`\`bash
cargo test                    # Unit tests
cargo test --test integration # Integration tests
./tests/e2e/run_all.sh       # E2E tests
\`\`\`

## Contributing

Contributions welcome! Please read CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines.

## License

MIT License - see LICENSE file for details.

## Acknowledgments

- Microsoft for the excellent Surface Dial hardware
- hidapi crate for cross-platform HID access
- rodio crate for cross-platform audio playback
\`\`\`

## README Requirements

- [x] Clear installation instructions for all platforms
- [x] Complete controls reference table
- [x] All CLI commands documented
- [x] Full configuration reference with all options
- [x] Feature list including all 10 planned features
- [x] Troubleshooting section for common issues
- [x] Building from source instructions

title: Platform detection module for installer
description:
# Description

Create a shell-based platform detection system for the unified installer script.

## Detection Logic

```bash
detect_platform() {
    local os=""
    local arch=""
    
    # OS detection
    case "$(uname -s)" in
        Darwin)  os="macos" ;;
        Linux)   os="linux" ;;
        MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*) os="windows" ;;
        *)       os="unknown" ;;
    esac
    
    # Architecture detection
    case "$(uname -m)" in
        x86_64|amd64)  arch="x86_64" ;;
        aarch64|arm64) arch="aarch64" ;;
        armv7l)        arch="armv7" ;;
        *)             arch="unknown" ;;
    esac
    
    echo "${os}_${arch}"
}
```

## Binary Naming Convention

```
surface-dial-macos-aarch64     # Apple Silicon
surface-dial-macos-x86_64      # Intel Mac
surface-dial-linux-x86_64      # Linux x64
surface-dial-linux-aarch64     # Linux ARM64
surface-dial-windows-x86_64.exe  # Windows
```

## Build Matrix

GitHub Actions should build for all platforms:

```yaml
# .github/workflows/release.yml
strategy:
  matrix:
    include:
      - os: macos-latest
        target: aarch64-apple-darwin
        name: macos-aarch64
      - os: macos-latest
        target: x86_64-apple-darwin
        name: macos-x86_64
      - os: ubuntu-latest
        target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
        name: linux-x86_64
      - os: ubuntu-latest
        target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
        name: linux-aarch64
      - os: windows-latest
        target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
        name: windows-x86_64
```

- `install.sh` - Main installer with platform detection
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` - Cross-platform build

title: Epic: Cross-Platform Installer & CLI System
description:
# Overview

Complete cross-platform installer and CLI configuration system for Surface Dial Volume Controller.

## Goals

1. **Cross-Platform Installer** - Single script that detects OS and installs appropriately
   - macOS: Homebrew-friendly, launchd for auto-start
   - Linux: systemd user service for auto-start
   - Windows: Task Scheduler for auto-start

2. **CLI Configuration System** - Agent-friendly CLI for runtime configuration
   - Volume step sizes (min/max)
   - Acceleration curve parameters
   - Double-click timeout
   - Long-press duration
   - Mic mode duration

3. **Quick-Start Help** - Beautifully formatted console output (giil-style)
   - Box-drawing characters
   - Color-coded sections
   - Platform-specific instructions

## Non-Goals

- GUI configuration (CLI only)
- Tray icon / menu bar integration (future work)

## Dependencies

This epic has no blockers - it extends a working Surface Dial implementation.

## Success Criteria

- `curl -fsSL <url> | bash` installs and auto-starts on macOS/Linux
- PowerShell equivalent for Windows
- `surface-dial --help` shows beautiful quick-start
- `surface-dial config set volume.step.max 10` works
- Coding agents can configure all settings programmatically
